metadata.title: The US fiscal trap — interest now exceeds defense, and why it is the macro root of the control thesis
metadata.status: facts from CBO/Treasury/PGPF/Fortune; the 'fiscal repression is the why behind the stablecoin + digital-ID rails' framing is a labeled interpretation that ties existing blocks together
metadata.frame: The macro keystone the corpus had spread across other blocks. The US ran its debt to ~$38-39T with FY2026 interest ~$1.0T - now LARGER than national defense. An over-indebted sovereign whose interest bill exceeds its military has only repressive ways out: inflate it away, find captive buyers, cap real yields, and control capital flight. That is the WHY behind the stablecoin->Treasury rail (engineered captive buyer), the Fed-follows-the-bond-market reading, the gold exit, and the programmable-money/digital-ID control rail (fiscal repression needs an enforcement layer). Overlay; excluded from the proofs. Ties together macro-stablecoin-treasury-rail, macro-gold-silver-reprice, macro-jobs-inflation-fed (the Fed trap), digitalid-orchestration-real-incentive.

the_numbers.debt: US national debt ~$38.4T (early 2026), heading toward ~$39T. status: fact.
the_numbers.interest_exceeds_defense: FY2026 net interest is projected ~$1.0T - MORE than national defense (~$947B). In Q1-FY2026, interest ($270B) already outpaced defense ($267B). At ~$88B/month, interest now equals US spending on defense AND education combined. status: fact (CBO/Treasury/PGPF).
the_numbers.trajectory: Interest costs hit an all-time high of $476B in 2022 and roughly DOUBLED to ~$1T by 2026; past borrowing now drives future deficits regardless of new policy - a compounding dynamic. status: fact.

why_it_is_a_trap: Once interest > defense and the debt is rolling at higher rates, the sovereign's options narrow to forms of FINANCIAL REPRESSION: (1) inflate the real value of the debt away (understate inflation - macro-jobs-inflation-fed, macro-official-data-integrity); (2) hold real yields below growth via a captive/managed buyer base (the Fed and now legislated stablecoin demand); (3) prevent capital flight that would force yields up. Raising rates to fight inflation now directly worsens the interest bill - a self-tightening trap (the Fed has no feasible single rate, macro-fed-trap / F1-F3 UNSAT). status: fact (mechanism) + interpretation (the 'trap' label).

the_repression_toolkit_already_documente.captive_buyer: The GENIUS Act routes stablecoin reserves into T-bills - a legislated, captive, conflicted buyer engineered to fund the front end as foreign sovereigns step back (macro-stablecoin-treasury-rail; Bessent's stated motive: 'rein in the national debt').
the_repression_toolkit_already_documente.foreign_exit_to_gold: Foreign central banks (China shed ~$86B) are diversifying OUT of Treasuries INTO gold (macro-gold-silver-reprice) - the symmetry: the US legislates a crypto-dollar buyer to replace the sovereigns who are leaving.
the_repression_toolkit_already_documente.the_enforcement_layer: Programmable money + mandatory digital ID is the executable enforcement layer for repression - it makes 'you cannot move your savings out, and they will be inflated/taxed on schedule' actually enforceable. This is the structural incentive the BIS stated on the record (digitalid-orchestration-real-incentive: 'absolute control ... and the technology to enforce that').
the_repression_toolkit_already_documente.measurement: Understating inflation lowers TIPS/COLA/bracket costs and flatters real growth - the fiscal motive behind the data-integrity thread (macro-official-data-integrity, Boskin).

the_synthesis: The fiscal trap is the ROOT that unifies the macro and control halves of the whole project. The AI bubble is the cyclical story; the debt trap is the secular one underneath it - and they meet at the Treasury market: the AI build needs cheap capital, the sovereign needs cheap funding, and BOTH are sustained only by keeping real yields suppressed and buyers captive. When the corpus says the digital-ID/programmable-money push is about 'fiscal repression of an over-indebted system,' THIS is the over-indebted system. Interest > defense is the number that makes the control rail rational for the state, independent of any conspiracy.

sources.name: Fortune - US government spending $88B/month in interest, equal to defense + education combined
Peterson Foundation (PGPF) - Interest costs on the national debt at all-time highs
IndexBox - Interest on national debt now 2nd-largest US expense ($179B in FY2026 Q1)
Fortune - Interest on US debt is a top driver of future deficits
EPIC for America - Interest spending tracker, Q1 FY2026
